So that is how you create your ads. All right. So after you launch your campaign, you created your campaign, you made all your different test ad sets, you created all your ads. Now we have to do abide by something called the four day law. So we set up our campaign, we set up our ad sets, we set up our ads and now we're just waiting to see if it works. Now, in the very beginning, it can be super tempting even for myself not to mess with it. Right. You see something doesn't look like it's going to work and you want to turn it off because you don't want to lose money or you see something that's working really good and you want to up the budget because you think it's going to make you a lot of money. But according to the four day law, you cannot touch any of your ad sets for four days. So even if one's just tanking, it's losing you tons of money. You cannot turn it off for four days. And even if one's making you a ton of money, you can't raise the budget for four days. Now, the reason why is because something called initial conditions. Now, in the beginning ads are just kind of figuring themselves out that Facebook algorithm, which is super complex, more complex than we can ever imagine, is trying to figure out which direction to take your ad sets, who to show your ads to, who's most likely to buy. And you don't know if it's working or not till after four days. An ad that's absolutely just tanking, losing tons of money might kind of get in the groove by the fourth day and just start rocking and making you tons of money. And an ad that looks profitable from day one might just tank on day four. So we have to wait for four days to figure out which ones are truly working and which ones aren't because the data from a day or two days is worthless. It doesn't mean anything. So do not touch your ads for the first four days. Whenever you create a new ad, this goes forever. Whenever you create a new ad set or new ad, you cannot touch them for four days until you see what happens, which again, is why I said there can't be any last ditch efforts because if you have your last $20, you're just going to turn the ads off. If you have your last $20 and you see one's working, you're going to height the price up. So you need to be methodical about it. You need to be super, like just step by step methodical, knowing exactly what you're going to do. That way your outcome can be predictable. Once it starts working, all right. So you're starting to get momentum. Your ad sets are starting to make you a little bit of money. You're sending traffic to your website. Now, once you've had 200 plus conversions, whether it's people who opted in on the first page or bought your low ticket product, whatever it is, once you've had 200 conversions, what we're going to do to take your advertising to the next level is create lookalike audiences. All right. Now, we have all these ad sets before and they're profitable, great. And our goal is to get as many profitable ads as possible. And that's what lookalike audiences are going to allow us to do. So here's what a lookalike audience is. So in the past, we had our conversions, people who went to our website and took the desired action. And we have that small group of maybe 200. What a lookalike audience is going to do is it's going to take Facebook's algorithm and it's going to find people just like the people who took the desired action on your page. So say you had 200 actions or 200 conversions, if Facebook's going to find a million people exactly like the people who converted on your website and there's like different levels of lookalike audiences. There's people who are 1% lookalike, a 2% lookalike, a 5% lookalike, and so on and so on. Now, the best ones obviously can be 1% because they're within 1% similarity to the people who already bought something or took the desired action on your website. And what this allows us to do is take our ads from guessing the targeting, guessing, oh, we think it's someone who likes Tony Robbins. Oh, we think it's someone who likes Dean Graziosi to the point where we're not guessing at all. Facebook knows more about these people than we could possibly ever guess. And now we're just going to let Facebook do its magic, do all the work for us. All right, now I'm going to show you guys how to create a lookalike audience and how to target them. All right, so here's how you create a custom audience inside of Facebook. So you're going to click on this little button up here. You're just going to go down to audiences. And then I'll take you to this page right here. Now, the reason creating custom audiences is so powerful is because Facebook can look at the people who have bought something on your website or who have opted in on your website, and it can look at 12,000 data points and compare them to everyone else on Facebook and find the people most similar to them. So you're not guessing on targeting anymore. You're not saying I think it's people who like Tony Robbins. Facebook's going to go and they're going to figure out, oh, it's this people who make this amount of money, who do this, who this, and they're going to find exactly who your target audience is for you without you have to do any of the work. So here's how you create a custom audience or lookalike audience. You're going to click Custom Audience and you're going to do website traffic. Versus scaling, what we're going to do is almost the exact same thing, except when we duplicate it, we're going to hike the budgets way up. Right. So we hike the budgets way up.
